MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Feb-02-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT X CORP., SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO NAMED DEFENDANT's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Susan Engel's unopposed pro hac vice application is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Jan-31-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. SOPHIA M RIOS, Esq. of Berger Montague PC (Tel: 619-489-0300) for Plaintiff;
Atty. KATE BAXTER-KAUF, Esq. present as prospective counsel pro hac vice for plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing RE: PLAINTIFF HENRY YEH's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

The Court notes Counsel Baxter-Kauf has been admitted as Counsel Pro Hac Vice in six different cases. The Court inquires Counsel Baxter-Kauf as to whether she intends to sit for the California Bar exam. Counsel Baxter-Kauf explains in these instances, her cases were transferred to California and that she intends to sit for the California Bar exam if further cases get transferred to California.

The Court rules as follows: Kate Baxter-Kauf's unopposed Pro Hac Vice Application is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not Reported.(302/RBU)
